The sun set behind the mountains, casting a golden glow across the valley below.

Scientists have discovered a new species of deep-sea fish near the Mariana Trench. The creature, found at depths exceeding 8,000 meters, uses bioluminescence to attract prey in complete darkness. Researchers believe it may hold clues to how life adapts to extreme pressure and temperature.

The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain during the late eighteenth century, fundamentally transformed the way human beings lived and worked. Factories replaced cottage industries, steam power displaced manual labor, and cities swelled with workers migrating from rural areas in search of wages. While this period produced remarkable technological advances and generated unprecedented economic growth, it also gave rise to harsh working conditions, child labor, and severe urban poverty. Historians continue to debate whether the net effect on ordinary people was positive or negative, and the answer likely depends on which generation and which social class one examines. What remains undeniable is that no corner of the world was left untouched by its consequences.
The only way to do great work is to love what you do - Steve Jobs

It is not the strongest of the species that survives, nor the most intelligent, but the one most responsive to change. - Charles Darwin. In the middle of every difficulty lies opportunity. - Albert Einstein.

Twenty years from now you will be more disappointed by the things that you didn't do than by the ones you did do. So throw off the bowlines. Sail away from the safe harbor. Catch the trade winds in your sails. Explore. Dream. Discover. - Mark Twain. Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts. - Winston Churchill. Believe you can and you're halfway there. - Theodore Roosevelt. The purpose of our lives is to be happy. - Dalai Lama.

Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers. A peck of pickled peppers Peter Piper picked. If Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers, where's the peck of pickled peppers Peter Piper picked? How much wood would a woodchuck chuck if a woodchuck could chuck wood?

How much wood would a woodchuck chuck if a woodchuck could chuck wood? He would chuck, he would, as much as he could, and chuck as much wood as a woodchuck would if a woodchuck could chuck wood. Betty Botter bought some butter, but she said the butter's bitter. If I put it in my batter, it will make my batter bitter. But a bit of better butter will make my batter better. So she bought a bit of butter better than her bitter butter. I saw Susie sitting in a shoeshine shop. Where she sits she shines, and where she shines she sits. Kal raat main ek movie dekhne baitha tha, lekin poore din ki thakan ki wajah se mera dhyaan baar-baar bhatak raha tha. Thodi der baad mujhe neend aane lagi aur aadhi movie dekhte-dekhte hi main so gaya. Ab baaki movie mujhe aaj ya weekend par dekhni padegi.

Aaj subah main thoda late utha, isliye mujhe jaldi-jaldi tayyar hona pada. Nashta bhi aaram se nahi kar paya aur sirf chai pi kar nikal gaya. Din bhar kaam mein kaafi busy raha aur kuch important tasks complete karne the. Shaam tak saara kaam khatam ho gaya, lekin tab tak kaafi thakan ho chuki thi. Ghar aakar thodi der aaram kiya, family ke saath baat ki aur phir kal ke kaam ki planning karke din khatam kiya.

Dear Mr. Sharma, I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to follow up on our discussion regarding the Q3 budget allocation. As per our agreement, the finance team will submit the revised figures by Thursday, 29th May. Should you require any additional information prior to the review meeting, please do not hesitate to reach out. Warm regards, Ankit Verma, Senior Manager - Finance Operations.

Subject: Strategic Review - FY2026 Roadmap Alignment. Dear Team, Following last week's leadership offsite, I wanted to consolidate the key takeaways and outline our path forward for the remainder of the fiscal year. Our primary focus areas will be revenue diversification, operational efficiency, and talent retention. Each business unit head is requested to submit a revised OKR document by June 15th, clearly mapping team objectives to the three pillars mentioned above. The PMO will schedule bi-weekly syncs beginning the first week of July to track progress against milestones. Please ensure that all dependencies are flagged in advance to avoid bottlenecks during execution. I appreciate your continued dedication and look forward to a strong close to FY2026. Best regards, Priya Malhotra, Chief Operating Officer.

SELECT name, email FROM users WHERE active = 1 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10;

SELECT u.name, COUNT(o.id) AS total_orders, SUM(o.amount) AS total_spent
FROM users u
JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
WHERE o.status = 'completed'
  AND o.created_at >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 90 DAY)
GROUP BY u.id, u.name
HAVING total_orders >= 3
ORDER BY total_spent DESC;

WITH monthly_revenue AS (
	SELECT
	DATE_FORMAT(o.created_at, '%Y-%m') AS month,
	p.category,
	SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue
	FROM orders o
	JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id
	JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id
	WHERE o.status = 'completed'
	  AND o.created_at BETWEEN '2025-01-01' AND '2025-12-31'
	GROUP BY month, p.category
),
ranked AS (
	SELECT
	month,
	category,
	revenue,
	RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY month ORDER BY revenue DESC) AS rnk
	FROM monthly_revenue
)
SELECT month, category, revenue
FROM ranked
WHERE rnk <= 3
ORDER BY month ASC, rnk ASC;

The sun was setting behind the hills as people slowly returned home after a long day.
A small bird landed on the fence and watched the quiet street for a few moments.

The city park was unusually busy this weekend. Families gathered near the lake, children rode bicycles along the pathways, and street musicians entertained visitors with cheerful melodies throughout the afternoon.
Learning a new skill often feels challenging at first. Progress may seem slow, but consistent practice builds confidence and gradually turns difficult tasks into familiar routines.

The research team spent several months collecting data from different regions before beginning their analysis. After reviewing thousands of responses, they identified patterns that revealed how small changes in daily habits could significantly improve productivity and overall well-being. Their findings were later presented at a conference where experts discussed practical ways to apply the results in workplaces and educational institutions.

When the old library underwent renovation, many residents worried that its historical character would be lost. Instead, architects carefully preserved the original structure while introducing modern facilities that improved accessibility and comfort. The project transformed the building into a vibrant community space that welcomed students, researchers, and local families alike.
Success is the result of small efforts repeated day after day.
The best way to predict the future is to create it.
Great achievements rarely happen overnight; they are built through patience, persistence, and the willingness to improve a little every day.
Challenges are not obstacles to success but opportunities to develop the skills and resilience required to reach it.

Growth begins when you step beyond what feels comfortable and familiar. Every meaningful accomplishment requires a period of uncertainty where progress is not always visible, yet every effort contributes to the result.

The distance between where you are and where you want to be is often crossed through consistency rather than intensity. Small actions performed regularly can achieve results that occasional bursts of effort never will.

from collections import Counter

text = "the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog"
words = text.split()
counts = Counter(words)

for word, count in counts.items():
    print(f"{word}: {count}")

most_common = counts.most_common(2)
print(most_common)

SELECT * FROM users;
SELECT name, email FROM customers;

SELECT c.customer_name, o.order_id, o.total_amount
FROM customers c
INNER JOIN orders o
ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
WHERE o.total_amount > 500
ORDER BY o.total_amount DESC;

SELECT p.product_name,
       SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_sold,
       SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue
FROM products p
JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
GROUP BY p.product_name
HAVING SUM(oi.quantity) > 10
ORDER BY revenue DESC;
